item.title: Tenerife prepares to evacuate hantavirus cruise ship
item.description: Passengers with symptoms could be sent to isolation on the island, health officials warn

item.title: Drone start-up Helsing set for $18bn valuation as investors pile into defence
item.description: German company backed by Spotify’s Daniel Ek set to raise $1.2bn in latest funding round

item.title: Iowa pension chief resigns amid concerns about performance figures
item.description: Former risk officer alleges executives used misleading benchmarks and risk measures to improve results

item.title: New York Stock Exchange to open private members’ club on Wall Street
item.description: Exclusive social hub planned for renovated vault as NYSE competes with Nasdaq for lucrative tech IPOs

item.title: NBA Europe’s path to profit
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: what a boutique bank takeover says about sports

item.title: WHSmith’s new owner makes a contrarian bet on the UK high street
item.description: Modella Capital plans to cut as many as 150 WHSmith stores out of the 480 it acquired and bring in new goods and services

item.title: Standard Life and CVC square the circle on defined-benefit pensions
item.description: There are more than £1tn of assets sitting in UK retirement schemes that could be picked up in risk transfer deals

item.title: Europe’s €14tn cash pile benefits banks not retail investors, BlackRock warns
item.description: World’s largest asset manager says governments must address under-investment of retail money in capital markets

item.title: Retail investors turn away from commercial property holdings
item.description: Rising interest rates and lower demand have hit returns in the past decade

item.title: The sailors who keep ‘ghost’ tankers moving
item.description: Working on an oil tanker under sanctions was a job like any other — until they ended up in the line of fire

item.title: Housebuilders scale back as Iran war and interest rates mute demand
item.description: Cuts to earnings guidance and land purchases cast doubt on whether government will meet 1.5mn new homes target

item.title: The worst team in the NBA is perfectly happy to lose
item.description: The Washington Wizards have been ‘tanking’. It may help them secure one of basketball’s next great players

item.title: A morning with Shoair Mavlian, director of The Photographers’ Gallery
item.description: How to survive — and thrive — as an arts organisation at a time of AI imagery, funding cuts and ‘woke’ backlash

item.title: Goldman’s big board bet
item.description: Bank’s management committee has swelled to a much larger size than those of its competitors

item.title: Brazil ends IPO drought as Iran war fuels stock market rally
item.description: São Paulo exchange emerges as indirect beneficiary of turmoil in the Gulf

item.title: Turkey unveils new ICBM — touted as able to hit the US mainland
item.description: Promotional AI video showed Yıldırımhan missile seemingly striking nuclear sites in America well beyond its range

item.title: Jane Street earned $10bn in first quarter as it doubled trading revenue
item.description: Results cement proprietary firm as one of the most profitable on Wall Street

item.title: LNG carriers test Iran blockade in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Pakistan cancelled plans to buy cargoes on spot market because it expected Gulf shipments to resume

item.title: Billionaire James Packer among backers of Architect’s OnlyFans deal
item.description: Australian mogul’s funds to invest in Architect Capital SPV taking a minority stake in platform

item.title: While AI pumps up portfolios, some companies sound the alarm
item.description: The clout of Big Tech groups is obscuring the precarious state of others

item.title: Pentagon publishes dozens of files on alleged UFOs
item.description: The public can ‘make up their own minds’ on strange sightings, says US defence department

item.title: Porsche cuts 500 jobs and closes its electric bike motor division
item.description: Luxury car maker retrenching to focus on core sports vehicle business

item.title: David Einhorn wants ladies in ‘sexy party clothes’ at his hedge fund party
item.description: Sorry, what year is it?

item.title: Ryan Cohen, GameStop’s rebel CEO
item.description: His $56bn hostile bid for eBay has baffled Wall Street, but it could be the world’s first meme stock-driven deal

item.title: Activist Oasis builds its Capita stake to 15% before AGM
item.description: Hong Kong-based investor had steadily increased its exposure to UK outsourcer via swaps

item.title: Chris Hohn’s hedge fund slashes $8bn Microsoft stake in warning over AI disruption
item.description: TCI has cut its position in tech giant from 10% to 1%

item.title: UBS boss warns of European decline with ‘over-regulation across the board’
item.description: Sergio Ermotti tells FT it would take a ‘very profound and painful crisis’ to pressure politicians to take action

item.title: Commerzbank to cut 3,000 jobs as it seeks to fend off UniCredit
item.description: Italian bank has built a roughly 30% stake in Germany’s second-largest listed lender

item.title: Uber-backed electric bike start-up Lime files for IPO
item.description: Operator of bright green e-bikes and scooters plans US listing this year

item.title: The fusion power race is heating up
item.description: Start-ups ride a funding wave as governments eye transformative potential
